concerns.text: Task T034 sets MBB block length to '1 quarter (3 months)', violating FR-006 and SC-004 which explicitly mandate 'block length=4 weeks'. This semantic weakening of the validation metric invalidates the robustness check defined in the spec.

Tasks T025, T029, and T026 fragment the Johansen cointegration logic into a manual decision gate (write temp file -> read/decide -> fit). This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and FR-013 by introducing potential non-deterministic human intervention, whereas the spec requires an automated system selection based on trace statistics.

Task T052 delegates the resolution of '[deferred]' masking proportions to a runtime task. This violates the requirement for a fully automated, reproducible pipeline (Constitution Principle I) and leaves Success Criteria SC-006 unmeasurable until a human intervenes to set the range, effectively weakening the spec's automation constraint.

Task T036a sources NBER recession dates from a generic landing page ('https://www.nber.org/research/business-cycle-dating') rather than a specific, canonical CSV/JSON endpoint. This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle II (Verified Accuracy) by failing to ensure a checksummed, canonical data source for the recession periods used in validation.

Task T028 implements VIF diagnostics but omits the mandatory action defined in the spec's Edge Cases: 'frame results as a joint relationship rather than independent effects'. The task description only mentions logging, which is a semantic weakening of the requirement to actively handle the collinearity in the output framing.
